title: Hybrid Work Productivity
description: How can professionals maintain high productivity and well‑being while working in a hybrid remote/office environment?
narrative: The user wants actionable strategies for balancing schedules across home and office, selecting collaboration tools, managing interruptions, and measuring performance without sacrificing work‑life balance. Relevance is judged by relevance to productivity metrics, time‑management techniques, workspace ergonomics, and evidence‑based best practices for hybrid models.

title: Solar+Battery Synergy
description: Which solar panel and battery combinations deliver the most reliable backup power for an off‑grid household?
narrative: The user is a homeowner planning a fully off‑grid solar system. They need concise, comparative information on compatible solar panels (incl. MPPT trackers), battery technologies (Li-ion vs. lead‑acid), inverter options, and typical lifecycles, as well as cost per peak watt and overall system sizing guidelines. Relevance is judged by whether the content directly addresses integration choices, performance metrics, and long‑term reliability rather than general solar facts.

title: Urban Green Space Health
description: What health benefits do residents gain from living near urban green spaces?
narrative: The user seeks evidence that links proximity to parks or green corridors with measurable health outcomes, focusing on studies that quantify impacts such as reduced stress, improved air quality, and increased physical activity, and wants to understand which factors—size, accessibility, maintenance—mediate these effects.

title: Climate Policy Effectiveness
description: What evidence exists that national carbon pricing policies have achieved measurable emissions reductions in the EU?
narrative: The user wants to assess whether EU nations' carbon pricing mechanisms (carbon taxes, ETS) have demonstrably lowered greenhouse gas emissions, considering empirical studies, data, and policy evaluations; relevant documents are those presenting quantitative before‑and‑after analyses, peer‑reviewed reports, or case studies that directly link pricing to emission changes, regardless of policy type.

title: Outpatient Antibiotic Stewardship
description: How can clinicians in outpatient practices improve antibiotic stewardship to reduce resistance while maintaining patient safety?
narrative: The user is a primary care provider seeking evidence-based strategies—including guidelines, interventions, and educational resources—to implement stewardship programs, measure outcomes, and address barriers specific to office-based settings. Relevant documents should present practical implementation steps, measurable impact data, and contextual adaptation, rather than purely theoretical discussion.

title: AI Ethics in Autonomous Vehicles
description: What are the primary ethical concerns and regulatory frameworks surrounding the deployment of autonomous vehicles in urban environments?
narrative: The user seeks recent scholarly articles or policy reports that discuss ethical dilemmas, stakeholder perspectives, and legal guidelines related to self-driving cars in cities, focusing on safety, privacy, liability, and social impact.

title: Emerging vector-borne diseases
description: What recent global trends exist in emerging vector-borne diseases, and how are environmental changes influencing their spread?
narrative: The user seeks peer-reviewed research, surveillance reports, and data analyses that identify new vector-borne disease emergences, examine how climate, land-use, and human mobility impact incidence, and evaluate risk assessment models for public health planning.

title: Urban Vertical Farms
description: What are the best practices for designing and operating vertical farms in dense city environments?
narrative: A city planner seeks detailed guidance on environmental controls, crop selection, supply chain integration, and economic viability specific to vertical farming systems; relevant documents should include case studies, technical standards, and ROI data.

title: Urban Green Roofs
description: What are the environmental and economic benefits of installing green roofs in mid-size cities?
narrative: The user seeks evaluation studies that quantify air quality improvement, energy savings, and cost–benefit analyses of green roofs within urban contexts, specifically focusing on cities with populations between 200,000 and 1 million. Relevance requires recent peer‑reviewed research published in the last ten years and empirical data, excluding theoretical or anecdotal reports.

title: Privacy-Preserving Health ML
description: What methods preserve privacy when training machine learning models on sensitive health data?
narrative: The user seeks academic and industry resources that explain techniques such as differential privacy, federated learning, and secure multi-party computation, emphasizing practical implementation details, performance trade-offs, and real-world case studies.

title: Renewable Adoption Impact
description: How does climate policy influence the adoption rate of renewable energy technologies across developing countries?
narrative: The user seeks to uncover the causal relationship between specific climate policy initiatives and the measurable increase in renewable energy uptake. Relevance is judged by inclusion of empirical studies, country-level adoption data, and policy case analyses; purely opinion pieces, general statements, or data outside the developing‑country context are excluded.

title: Homemade Flea Repellents
description: How effective are homemade flea repellents compared to commercial products in preventing flea infestations on pets?
narrative: The user is a pet owner seeking safe, eco‑friendly alternatives to commercial flea control. They need evidence of efficacy, safety for animals and humans, application methods, and long‑term prevention. Relevant documents must include peer‑reviewed studies, veterinary guidelines, consumer reports, and ingredient analyses that directly compare homemade solutions with commercial flea control products.

title: Urban Heat Island Mitigation
description: I want to research strategies to mitigate the urban heat island effect in metropolitan areas.
narrative: The user is looking for practical, evidence‑based methods for reducing urban heat islands, prioritizing studies that present case analyses, temperature monitoring data, and documented implementation results, while excluding purely theoretical or non‑empirical discussions.

title: Smart Home Energy Efficiency
description: What are the most effective strategies to reduce residential energy consumption using smart home technologies?
narrative: Seeking detailed comparative analysis of smart thermostat integration, appliance optimization, and home automation schedules, with emphasis on cost‑effectiveness and measurable power savings in U.S. households.

title: Urban Renewable Adoption
description: What are the principal barriers and incentives for implementing renewable energy technologies in major metropolitan areas?
narrative: The user seeks contextual insights into how regulatory frameworks, economic incentives, and technical challenges shape renewable energy uptake in cities. Relevant documents should provide case studies, policy analyses, and techno-economic assessments specific to urban environments; generic overviews of renewable technologies or rural deployment strategies should be considered irrelevant.

title: Bike Lanes Noise
description: What are the specific noise impact metrics associated with urban bike lanes in medium‑size cities?
narrative: The user is an urban planner researching how bike lane placement influences traffic noise. Relevance requires empirical studies that report decibel levels, traffic density, lane width, or mitigation techniques, and that are specific to cities with populations between 100,000 and 500,000.

title: Urban Agriculture
description: How do city-based farms contribute to local food security and community sustainability?
narrative: The user seeks an overview of the role that urban farming plays in ensuring food availability within metropolitan areas, including metrics on yield, workforce development, and ecological impact. Relevant documents should discuss case studies, statistical evidence, and policy implications related to urban agriculture programs, but not general gardening advice or unrelated horticulture topics.

title: Sustainable Food Logistics
description: What are effective strategies to cut the carbon footprint of perishable food transport from farms to retail outlets?
narrative: The user is looking for evidence‑based guidance on logistics practices, technologies, and policies that reduce greenhouse‑gas emissions in the cold‑chain supply of perishable foods. Relevant documents should discuss route optimisation, energy‑efficient refrigeration, packaging innovations, carbon‑accounting methods, and regulatory or incentive frameworks that influence sustainable distribution.

title: Heat Island Mitigation
description: What are the most effective strategies for reducing urban heat island effects in mid-sized cities?
narrative: The user wants to understand actionable approaches for mitigating urban heat island (UHI) impacts, focusing on proven interventions such as green roofs, reflective surfaces, vegetation corridors, and policy measures. Relevance should include peer‑reviewed studies, city case plans, and evaluation reports that quantify temperature reductions, energy savings, or health benefits. Non‑technical general articles or data lacking evaluation are not relevant.

title: Quantum Memory Optimization
description: How can quantum algorithms be optimized to use minimal memory while maintaining performance?
narrative: The user is researching advanced techniques—such as error‑correction codes, qubit recycling, and memory‑efficient circuit designs—to reduce memory overhead in quantum computations without compromising computational speed. Relevance criteria include any document that presents theoretical frameworks, algorithmic strategies, or empirical benchmarks demonstrating practical memory savings in quantum systems.

title: Lunar Eclipse Forecast
description: How can an amateur astronomer predict and prepare for upcoming lunar eclipses, including dates, visibility zones, atmospheric impact, and best observation practices?
narrative: The user is an amateur astronomer seeking a comprehensive guide to planning lunar eclipse observations over the next few years. Relevant documents should present accurate ephemerides, sky visibility maps, atmospheric considerations, and practical advice on equipment, timing, and location. Irrelevant material such as general astronomy background or unrelated scientific topics should be excluded.

title: Mindfulness Productivity
description: How does practicing mindfulness meditation impact employee productivity in corporate settings?
narrative: The user seeks evidence-based analysis of daily or weekly mindfulness practices and measurable productivity outcomes, such as task completion rates, error rates, or self-reported focus. Relevant documents should cite peer-reviewed studies from 2010 onward, provide sample sizes, statistical significance, and discuss limitations or confounding factors. Exclude anecdotal reports, non-academic sources, and studies unrelated to corporate or workplace environments.

title: Urban Food Deserts
description: What are the main socioeconomic factors contributing to the persistence of food deserts in mid‑size American cities, and how have policy interventions affected access over the past decade?
narrative: A graduate student seeks scholarly articles, policy analyses, and case studies that explore the socioeconomic drivers of urban food deserts—including income disparity, transportation barriers, zoning regulations, and demographic shifts—and evaluate the effectiveness of interventions (e.g., subsidies, mobile markets, zoning revisions) implemented between 2014 and 2024. Relevance requires peer‑reviewed research, official reports, or comprehensive evaluations that provide data, theoretical explanations, or empirical evidence on both causes and policy impact.

title: Immigrant Entrepreneurship
description: What are the key success factors for first‑generation immigrant entrepreneurs achieving sustainable small business growth in urban settings?
narrative: The user seeks analytical evidence, case studies, and empirically grounded insights on strategies, challenges, and support mechanisms that enable immigrant founders to establish profitable, resilient businesses in major metros, excluding purely anecdotal or opinion pieces.

title: CPAP Reimbursement Eligibility
description: What Medicare Part B and Medicaid policies cover CPAP equipment reimbursement, and what are the eligibility criteria for patients with sleep apnea?
narrative: The user is looking to determine whether CPAP machines and associated accessories are reimbursed under Medicare Part B or Medicaid, including which tests or documentation are required. Relevant documents should provide details on covered equipment types, eligibility thresholds, patient condition requirements, cost‑sharing rules, and any policy distinctions between Medicare and Medicaid. The answer should reflect current policy language and highlight any recent updates or exceptions that affect coverage.

title: Splicing Mechanism Comparison
description: What are the key differences between eukaryotic and prokaryotic RNA splicing mechanisms?
narrative: The user seeks a detailed comparison of molecular processes underlying intron removal and exon splicing in eukaryotic cells versus the absence of splicing in prokaryotes, focusing on spliceosome components, splice site recognition, exon definition, regulatory elements, and the functional consequences for gene expression.

title: Remote Work Cognition
description: What are the effects of remote work on employees' cognitive performance and mental wellbeing?
narrative: An organization wants to evaluate how remote work policies may impact employee cognition and mental health, and is looking for peer‑reviewed studies, meta‑analyses and empirical data that assess changes in attention, executive function, stress, and burnout among remote versus office workers. Relevance requires research published after 2015, focusing on working adults, and presenting quantitative evidence.

title: Telehealth Chronic Pain
description: What evidence exists for best practices in implementing telehealth services to manage chronic pain patients, focusing on clinical effectiveness, patient satisfaction, and financial viability?
narrative: The user is a health system administrator looking for evidence-based guidelines and case studies on designing telehealth programs for chronic pain management; relevant documents should describe comparative effectiveness studies, patient engagement metrics, cost and reimbursement models, technology implementation details, and regulatory/compliance considerations.

title: Green Roof Economics
description: What is the cost‑benefit profile of installing green roofs on commercial buildings in mid‑latitude cities over a 10‑year horizon?
narrative: The user is looking for detailed economic analyses that include initial installation costs, ongoing maintenance, energy savings, resale value impact, and potential incentives, based on comparable case studies from the past decade. Relevant documents must provide quantitative data, clearly defined assumptions, and sensitivity analyses to variables such as climate and building type. Non‑quantitative opinions or general promotional material are considered irrelevant.

title: Banking Privacy Concerns
description: What are the main privacy concerns for consumers using online banking services?
narrative: The user seeks to understand potential privacy risks, data handling practices, and regulatory safeguards in online banking, focusing on data collection, third‑party sharing, and security measures, in order to assess which institutions best align with their privacy expectations.

title: Urban Green Roofs
description: What are the best practices for maximizing biodiversity on green roofs in large metropolitan areas?
narrative: The user is an urban planner or environmental scientist seeking detailed guidance on creating green roofs that support diverse plant and animal species. Relevance requires documents that discuss ecological frameworks, species selection, soil and substrate management, irrigation strategies, maintenance schedules, and case studies of successful biodiversity‑enhanced green roofs in city settings. Articles that focus solely on structural engineering, cost analysis, or aesthetic design without reference to ecological benefits are not considered relevant.

title: Remote Work Wellbeing
description: How has the shift to remote work impacted employees' digital wellbeing and what strategies can mitigate negative effects?
narrative: The user seeks an overview of research on digital wellbeing in remote settings, including studies, interventions, and best‑practice recommendations, prioritizing evidence‑based sources and recent findings.

title: Alcohol Usage Demographics
description: How do alcohol consumption rates vary across different demographic groups in the United States?
narrative: The user is seeking evidence‑based analyses or statistical reports that provide per‑capita alcohol consumption, average weekly intake, and percentile rankings broken down by demographic variables such as age, race/ethnicity, gender, and socioeconomic status. Relevant sources should be reputable (e.g., government agencies, peer‑reviewed journals, national surveys) and include direct comparisons or trend data. Documents that merely mention alcohol consumption in a general sense, focus on unrelated marketing or entertainment topics, or lack a clear demographic breakdown are considered irrelevant.

title: Deployed vs Non‑Deployed Veteran Suicide Trends
description: What are the differences in suicide rates and trends between deployed and non‑deployed veterans from 2005 to 2011?
narrative: The user seeks detailed, longitudinal evidence on how deployment status influences suicide risk among U.S. veterans. Relevant documents should present statistical comparisons, include cohort sizes, year‑by‑year rates, and discuss potential contributing factors such as age, gender, and PTSD exposure. Exclusion criteria include studies not specifying deployment status, reports that only provide aggregate national data without disaggregation, or articles not published in peer‑reviewed journals.

title: Business Renewable Incentives
description: What state-level tax credits, rebates, and subsidies are available for small businesses adopting renewable energy solutions in the United States?
narrative: The user is a small business owner looking to reduce energy costs and qualify for financial incentives. Documents should describe specific state incentive programs, eligibility criteria, application processes, and recent changes. Exclude generic commercial energy advice and federal programs not tied to state incentives.

title: Adolescent Social Media
description: What mental health effects does prolonged social media use have on adolescents, and what interventions can mitigate negative outcomes?
narrative: The user is looking for evidence-based research on the psychological impact of social media use among individuals aged 12-18, including both short- and long-term effects, as well as effective prevention and intervention strategies. Relevance criteria include peer-reviewed studies, systematic reviews, meta-analyses, and intervention guidelines from reputable health organizations.

title: Urban Heat Mitigation
description: What are effective strategies and best practices for reducing urban heat islands in mid-sized cities?
narrative: The user is researching practical, evidence‑based approaches to mitigate urban heat island effects, especially in cities with populations between 200,000 and 1,000,000. Relevant documents should include case‑studies, implementation guidelines, measured temperature reductions, and policy recommendations that have been applied in comparable urban settings.

title: Mortgage Credit Impact
description: How has a customer's credit score history over the past decade affected the mortgage rates they receive?
narrative: The user seeks a nuanced analysis of how a borrower’s long‑term credit history—specifically trends and score changes over the last ten years—influences mortgage rates offered by lenders, requiring documents that provide longitudinal data, market shift context, and lender‑specific policy analysis.

title: Airport Code Confusion
description: What distinguishes IATA, ICAO, and other local codes for European airports and how can I reliably interpret them?
narrative: The user wants clear, authoritative guidance on interpreting and applying different airport identifiers (IATA, ICAO, and local codes) to avoid booking or travel planning errors.

title: Adolescent Mental Health Interventions
description: How effective are school‑based multimodal interventions (e.g., cognitive‑behavioral therapy, psychoeducation, peer support) at reducing anxiety and depression among adolescents, and what factors mediate or moderate these outcomes?
narrative: The user wants to locate empirical studies that evaluate the impact of integrated mental‑health programs within urban school settings, comparing different intervention components and measuring anxiety/depression outcomes. Relevance criteria include peer‑reviewed research published within the last 15 years, clearly defined multimodal components, defined population of adolescents (ages 12–18), measurable outcomes (e.g., validated anxiety/depression scales), and reports on mediators or moderators such as gender, socioeconomic status, or cultural background. The user also expects discussion of methodological quality, sample sizes, and policy implications.

title: Microplastic Impact on Marine Mammals
description: What recent research shows about how microplastic pollution affects the health of marine mammals?
narrative: I want to find peer‑reviewed scientific articles that present empirical data on microplastic ingestion, physiological stress markers, or mortality rates in marine mammals such as dolphins, whales, and seals. Documents that only provide anecdotal evidence or general environmental commentary are not relevant, nor are policy papers lacking a data‑driven assessment.

title: Licensing Implications of AI Models
description: What are the key legal and ethical considerations when deploying proprietary AI models in a multi‑jurisdictional environment?
narrative: The user seeks guidance on compliance frameworks, data residency requirements, and intellectual property constraints applicable to AI deployments across different countries, as well as best practices for mitigating regulatory risk.

title: Cheese Yield Analysis
description: What factors influence the cheese yield from milk and how can production be optimized?
narrative: The user is a dairy production manager seeking to understand the relationships among milk volume, cheese weight, and recipe variations, looking for data-driven insights and best practices for increasing yield while maintaining quality.
